intent: We are not building the Forge Agent architecture. We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture
scope_statement:
Build the complete Consensus Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent that uses dual-LLM consensus (Claude + GPT-4o with Claude arbitrating) to develop high-quality software autonomously from technical specifications. The system comprises a two-process architecture: a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID auth, Keychain secrets, XPC channel, process management per TRD-1) and a Python 3.12 backend (ConsensusEngine per TRD-2, 8-stage BuildPipeline with 3-pass review per TRD-3, GitHubTool per TRD-5, BuildLedger per TRD-4, DocumentStore with FAISS vector retrieval per TRD-10, HolisticReview per TRD-6, TRDWorkflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes per TRD-7), connected via authenticated Unix socket with line-delimited JSON (TRD-1 §6, TRD-12), with Mac CI runner infrastructure for Swift validation (TRD-9), comprehensive security controls (TRD-11), recovery and state management (TRD-13), iterative code quality pipeline (TRD-14), and full testing/validation strategy (TRD-16). The agent decomposes plain-language intent into ordered PRDs, generates PR specs, implements code via parallel consensus generation, runs iterative fix loops (up to 20 passes with escalating strategies), executes CI, and gates on operator approval at every merge point.

prd_plan.title: Product Identity, Branding, and Canonical Naming
Swift macOS Shell Architecture and Application Skeleton
Swift Authentication, Session Lifecycle, and Keychain Security
Authenticated Swift–Python IPC Channel and Runtime Handshake
Backend Process Management, Startup Ordering, Health Monitoring, and Graceful Shutdown
GitHub Integration Layer and Repository Bootstrap
Document Import, Parsing UX, and Project Document Lifecycle
Document Store, Embedding Pipeline, FAISS Retrieval, and Injection Defense
Consensus Engine Core, Provider Adapters, and Arbitration Logic
Build Ledger, Multi-Agent Coordination, and Dependency Journaling
Intent Intake, Scope Confirmation, and Ordered PRD Planning
PRD Generation and PR Specification Decomposition
Code Generation Stage and Native-Output Pre-Commit Quality Gates
Three-Pass Review, Security Scanning, and Unexpected URL Detection
Iterative 20-Pass Local Fix Loop and Test Growth Engine
CI Gate, Cross-Environment Validation, and Autonomous CI Fix Loop
macOS CI Runner Infrastructure and Swift Validation Pipeline
Operator Gates, Build Stream UX, and Context Panel Integration
Recovery, Checkpointing, Resume Flow, and State Durability
Security Control Framework and Threat Model Enforcement
TRD Workflow with FOUNDER, ENGINEER, and CONSULTANT Modes
Holistic Review Workflow and Review Fix PR Generation
Comprehensive Test Strategy, Coverage Enforcement, and Live Validation
Operational Hardening, Observability, Settings Migration, and Release Readiness

1.title: Add canonical product identity schema and naming policy documentation
1.summary: Create the foundational naming policy document and machine-readable canonical name specification. Defines 'Forge' as the primary product name, 'Dev Agent' as the subtitle, and all derived forms (PascalCase, kebab-case, SCREAMING_SNAKE, reverse-DNS bundle ID, etc.) with explicit rules for when each form is used. The JSON spec serves as the single source of truth that both Python and Swift constants modules are derived from. Includes a README explaining the naming hierarchy, migration rationale, and prohibited legacy variants.

2.title: Add Python branding constants module (`forge/branding.py`)
2.summary: Create the Python single source of truth for all product identity constants. Defines a frozen `Branding` dataclass loaded from / validated against `shared/product_identity/canonical_name_spec.json`. Attributes include: primary name ('Forge'), subtitle ('Dev Agent'), full display name ('Forge — Dev Agent'), PascalCase ('ForgeDevAgent'), kebab-case ('forge-dev-agent'), SCREAMING_SNAKE ('FORGE_DEV_AGENT'), bundle identifier prefix ('ai.yousource.forge'), Keychain service ID, branch prefix, commit prefix, CI workflow prefix, Sparkle appcast name, notification title, Dock label, menu bar label, log prefix, and bootstrap signal. Exposes a module-level singleton `BRANDING` and a `get_branding()` accessor. No external dependencies beyond Python stdlib.

3.title: Add Swift branding constants (`BrandingConstants.swift`)
3.summary: Create the Swift-side single source of truth for product identity in the macOS shell. Defines a caseless `enum BrandingConstants` with static let properties mirroring every form from the Python module: `displayName`, `subtitle`, `fullDisplayName`, `bundleIdentifierPrefix`, `keychainServiceID`, `menuBarLabel`, `dockLabel`, `notificationTitle`, `sparkleAppcastName`, `branchPrefix`, `commitPrefix`, `logPrefix`, `bootstrapSignal`. Values match the canonical spec JSON. Includes documentation comments on each constant. Placed in a shared Swift file importable by both the main app target and any XPC service target.

4.title: Add cross-language branding parity validation script
4.summary: Create a Python script (`scripts/validate_branding_parity.py`) that parses both `forge/branding.py` and `ForgeDevAgent/Sources/BrandingConstants.swift`, and also validates both against the canonical JSON spec. Asserts that every named constant shares the same value across all three sources. Extracts Python values via import and Swift values via regex parsing of static lets. Exits non-zero on any mismatch with a clear diff. Also provides a pytest wrapper (`tests/test_branding_parity.py`) so it participates in the normal test suite.

5.title: Add naming lint rule and prohibited-variants scanner
5.summary: Create a static analysis tool (`scripts/lint_branding.py`) that scans all `.py`, `.swift`, `.md`, `.yml`, `.yaml`, `.json`, `.toml`, and `.cfg` files for prohibited legacy name variants. Prohibited patterns: 'Consensus Dev Agent', standalone 'ForgeAgent' (without 'Dev'), standalone 'Forge Agent' (without 'Dev'), 'forge-agent' (should be 'forge-dev-agent'), 'FORGE_AGENT' (should be 'FORGE_DEV_AGENT'). Supports an allowlist file (`.branding-allowlist`) for intentional exceptions (migration comments, historical changelog). Reports violations with file path, line number, and suggested replacement. Exits non-zero on any unallowed violation. Includes unit tests exercising the scanner against fixture files.

6.title: Add Python naming rules for branches, labels, artifacts, and commit prefixes
6.summary: Implement standardized naming rule helpers in `forge/branding.py` (or a companion `forge/branding_rules.py`) for generating canonical branch prefixes, artifact names, workflow labels, commit prefixes, and generated file naming from the branding constants. These helpers are used by the consensus engine, build pipeline, GitHub tool, and TRD workflow modules. Includes tests validating each rule produces correct kebab-case, PascalCase, and scoped identifier forms.

7.title: Migrate Python backend references to use branding constants
7.summary: Audit and update all existing Python source files to import and use `forge.branding.BRANDING` and `forge.branding_rules` instead of hardcoded product name strings. Covers: log format strings and prefixes, XPC/socket bootstrap signal string, build ledger commit message prefixes, PR description templates and branch name generators, holistic review report headers and branch prefixes, TRD workflow mode labels, and user-facing error messages. Updates `.branding-allowlist` for any intentional legacy references that must remain.

8.title: Migrate Swift shell UI references to use BrandingConstants
8.summary: Audit and update all existing Swift source files to use `BrandingConstants` instead of hardcoded product name strings. Covers: menu bar label and tooltip text, Dock menu item titles, notification title and body templates, Touch ID authentication reason strings, Sparkle appcast configuration, XPC/socket bootstrap signal matching, NSLog/os_log format strings. Also creates a shared `.xcconfig` that defines `PRODUCT_NAME` and `BUNDLE_DISPLAY_NAME` from canonical values for Xcode build settings.

9.title: Migrate documentation and configuration files to canonical naming
9.summary: Update all Markdown documentation, YAML/TOML configuration, and CI workflow files to use canonical 'Forge' / 'Dev Agent' naming. Covers: README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, all TRD documents (TRD-1 through TRD-16), pyproject.toml (project name, description, script entry points), .github/workflows/*.yml (workflow names, job names, badge labels), CHANGELOG.md header, contributing guides (branch naming conventions updated to `forge-dev-agent/` prefix), and any other operator-facing text files.

10.title: Security review: Keychain service ID and bundle identifier migration
10.summary: Dedicated security-critical PR for migrating the Keychain service identifier and bundle identifier to the canonical naming scheme. Implements: (1) a Swift migration function that detects secrets stored under the old service ID and copies them to the new canonical service ID, (2) deletion of old Keychain entries only after successful copy and verification, (3) fallback read logic for the old service ID if the new one is empty (upgrade-in-place support), (4) updated bundle identifier in Info.plist and entitlements, (5) updated code signing identity references if they embed the bundle ID, (6) Python-side security identifier constants for socket/service labels and bootstrap environment markers. Isolated because incorrect Keychain migration can lock operators out of their API keys.

11.title: Add CI gate for branding lint and parity checks
11.summary: Add branding enforcement to the CI pipeline so no PR can merge with prohibited name variants or cross-language parity violations. Adds `.github/workflows/branding.yml` that runs: (1) `scripts/lint_branding.py` across the full repo, (2) `scripts/validate_branding_parity.py` for Swift/Python/JSON constant parity, (3) the full pytest branding test suite. Failures block the PR. The workflow uses canonical naming ('Forge Dev Agent — Branding Lint'). Also updates existing CI workflow files to use canonical naming in their display names and job labels.

12.title: Add backend naming drift scanner with AST-based hardcoded string detection
12.summary: Create an AST-based scanner (`scripts/scan_name_drift.py`) that goes beyond regex to detect hardcoded product name strings in Python source files that aren't routed through `forge.branding.BRANDING`. Walks the AST of all `.py` files, identifies string literals matching product name patterns, and checks whether they originate from a `BRANDING` attribute access. Supports the same allowlist as the lint scanner. Includes tests with fixture files containing both compliant and non-compliant code samples.

13.title: End-to-end product identity conformance integration tests
13.summary: Capstone integration test suite validating branding consistency across the full system. Tests: (1) golden file test — all `BRANDING` attributes match a checked-in fixture, (2) AST-based scan confirms no Python file contains hardcoded product names outside `BRANDING`, (3) XPC bootstrap signal matches between Python and Swift, (4) `pyproject.toml` project name matches `BRANDING.kebab_case`, (5) CI workflow file names and job names use canonical prefixes, (6) snapshot test of `.branding-allowlist` to prevent silent growth, (7) Keychain migration identifiers match canonical spec, (8) Swift bundle metadata matches canonical spec. Serves as the final regression gate against naming drift.
